canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, probing is consistently defined as a technique to assess whether specific properties or information can be decoded from a model's internal, latent representations. The method is operationalized by training an auxiliary classifier on these hidden states to predict the target property. For instance, one paper specifies training a 'linear classifier' on hidden representations ("CodeArena: Evaluating and AligningCodeLLMs on Human Preference"), while another employs a 'specialized two-layer readout' trained on a supervised dataset ("How Transformers Learn Structured Data: Insights From Hierarchical Filtering"). The stated purpose is to determine what information is encoded within the model, with one paper framing it as a 'mechanistic interpretability technique'. Specific applications mentioned include assessing the encoding of 'intermediate bridge entities' and confirming how 'computation going up the tree is distributed sequentially in the transformer blocks' by probing for 'ancestor symbols'.
papers: CodeArena: Evaluating and AligningCodeLLMs on Human Preference
How Do Large Vision-Language Models See Text in Image? Unveiling the Distinctive Role ofOCRHeads
How Transformers Learn Structured Data: Insights From Hierarchical Filtering
Position: We Need An Algorithmic Understanding of Generative AI

definitions.text: A method that trains auxiliary classifiers on internal model representations to assess the presence of specific information (e.g., ancestor symbols) at different network layers.
definitions.paper: How Transformers Learn Structured Data: Insights From Hierarchical Filtering

definitions.text: A technique to assess whether specific properties can be accurately decoded from a model's latent representations.
definitions.paper: Position: We Need An Algorithmic Understanding of Generative AI
